description: Create Part2_PhaseEngine.mqh - Symphony's Phase Engine (curvature-based impulse detection + Phases 1-4) and ARC v2 convexity exit system. This is the ENTRY generation core - kept verbatim from Symphony minus the DRDWCT risk engine.
steps: Create Part2_PhaseEngine.mqh at the repo root

Port UpdatePhaseEngine() verbatim from Symphony: pivot detection, SHORT impulse detection (last high -> new low > ATR * mult), LONG impulse detection (last low -> new high > ATR * mult), mode/anchor state, flipzone/inducement level finding, phase assignment for both SHORT side (retracement-based with pre-conv gating) and LONG side (same logic inverted), impulse invalidation on close beyond anchor, previous phase tracking

Port UpdateARC() verbatim from Symphony: LONG ARC calculation (from anchorLow projected with convexity power), SHORT ARC calculation (from anchorHigh projected with convexity power), horizon normalization

Port ManageArcInstitutionalExits() verbatim from Symphony: ARC exhaustion detection, institutional band calculation (inner/outer from inducement/anchor), outer-band sweep tracking per impulse, phase-change detection at extreme, composite exit condition (ARC exhaust + outer sweep + inner re-entry + phase-end), position closure execution for matching positions
acceptance_criteria: Part2_PhaseEngine.mqh exists with valid MQL5 syntax
UpdatePhaseEngine() matches Symphony logic exactly (pivot detection, impulse detection, phase assignment 1-4, invalidation)
UpdateARC() matches Symphony logic exactly (convexity arc projection)
ManageArcInstitutionalExits() matches Symphony logic exactly (composite exit conditions)
All functions reference global state from Part1_Core.mqh structs
verification: Read the file and verify logic matches symphony.txt sections 6, 6B, and 16
Verify no DRDWCT risk engine code is present
Verify proper use of global PhaseEngineState struct fields
